posts.text: Axis Fireball 1750 Brand New, never used and purchased new directly from Axis. Comes exactly as it would new with the protective cover and the rubber trailing edge protection. I have decided to go a different direction with my pump foiling. Save $200 over buying new. $1,420 firm. Local pick up near Edmonds, Venmo preferred.
posts.condition: New - still in bag with full protective cover
posts.scam_flags: NONE - appears legit. New in bag with cover, buying new price ~$1620, selling $1420. Legitimate change-of-direction reason.

posts.text: Hi Guys - What sort of rubber and glue do you use to replace the one on the baseplate?

key_comments.text: Someone stole my rubber base plate and I never got around to replacing it. Actually don't mind it without it now, in case you needed a plan B
I used rubber cement for a neighbor. Seems like it u keep mast attached to board this becomes an issue for some

posts.text: following on from the pumping question - here is my hot take after 10 days straight on the surge 1010. It will improve your pumping immensely. It redirects so easily - its a zen thing - think it and it happens. This means that the narrow stance, efficient, pump position becomes way way more natural and combining it with a flowy carve style is so, so rewarding. I also kinda forces you into the stacked position of feet centred below shoulders. If you are offset its really hard to pump without wiggling. Getting back onto home turf chaotic lumps riding in wellington boots really showed me how much it changed my style in 10 days.

key_comments.text: Can I ask you how much you weigh.

I've been swapping back and forth between the Surge and the Fireball a lot the last days trying to decide where my heart lies and for wing, parawing and Foil Drive action it is Surge all the way. The fireball is more efficient without a doubt and leaps forwards with every pump and size for size has a slightly better glide path BUT the Surge is so much more playful and forgiving of mistakes that it wins hands down for my riding style. The only exception is SUP foil downwind. Whilst I am a competent DW sup foiler paddling up still gets my heart beating and I tend to go for distance rather than flowy carvy type dw runs. In this the Fireball shows it's true colours (and dare I say it, justifies its place in the line up!)

posts.text: I kite on surge 740, adv+ ultrashort, p275. It's the most playful setup iv ever tried! Decided to try that setup with my FD assist+. I couldn't flat water start it, needed a little bump. Had great glide and super easy to pump for such a small foil but it was super twitchy especially controlling pitch. Any one tried similar setup, would a bigger stab help?

key_comments.text: I had the same problem with my surge 830 adv+ultra 320ss Put a 1 deg positive shim on the tail Settled down the twichness and had much better pitch control.

There is a huge difference between kite foiling and Foil Drive riding. The kite is like a 3rd leg.... get rid of it and you suddenly notice how unstable foiling can be. Not sure your weight but 740 is a small foil to start on a foil drive for most riders…

posts.text: Which wings are easy to pump on SUP? I'm 175lbs and currently SUP foil, para wing (+dw), and dockstart on the 1300v1. I also have an Art Pro 1201 and a 999v2 but I cannot consistently pump either of them for the life of me. I hear that the 1201 is supposed to be a pump machine, but as soon as I loose speed it'll drop out hard on me. I also can't flat water popup on it, but the 1300 is starting to feel real big in the surf and slow in the bumps. Is there a larger Surge coming?
posts.discipline: SUP foil, para wing, DW, dockstart

key_comments.text: The 1201 is a great sup surf foil that pumps amazing with the right stab. Your problem is too small a stab. With skinny 45 I can only pump 2-3 waves, with progressive 375 I can connect 10+ waves.

posts.unanswered_q: Is there a larger Surge coming?

posts.text: 80 kgs, looking to learn parawing on 90 litre midlength. Surge 1010 or Spitfire 1030/1100? I have the Surge 950, even if it gets up real easy I think it is on the small side for me to learn parawing?

key_comments.text: The spitfire is an awesome wing wing and has been for a few years now but the Surge has the benefit of several years more knowledge and R and D and is one of the new high camber foil designs. The early starting, pump and glide and of course playfulness…
1010 Surge is my light wind weapon for wingfoiling. I'm 85 kg. If your board gets you going easily enough on your 950, then Surge 1010 could be the ideal wing.

posts.text: Catching waves with lower motor pod in efoil position on axis integrated mast using fusion. Still can pump and stay on the bumps. Small 1-2ft shore break type of day.
Manawa Boogie Foiling - Fireball 880, Adv+ 640, Skinny 40, Ultra 900
